name: Madagascar (Republic of Madagascar / Repoblikan'i Madagasikara / République de Madagascar)
summary: Madagascar is an Indian Ocean civil-law republic whose family-law framework operates under the Civil Code (Code Civil) drawing on French civil-law substantive heritage with substantial codification of customary-law and Merina/Betsileo traditional matrimonial-property provisions (notably the 'fitampoha' marriage-property tradition). Family-law framework operates under Law 2007-022 on Marriage, Law 2007-023 on Filiation, Law 2007-024 on Parental Authority, and Law 2017-014 on Child Protection. Parental authority and child custody are governed by Law 2007-024 arts. 1-30. The Supreme Court (Cour Suprême) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the High Constitutional Court (Haute Cour Constitutionnelle) operates separate constitutional review. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Court of First Instance (Tribunal de Première Instance). Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministry of Public Health framework. Madagascar is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the interest-of-the-child standard. Madagascar acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 September 2004.

statutory_framework.title: Law on Parental Authority
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Law on Parental Authority codifying parental authority and child custody. Arts. 1-30 govern parental authority.

statutory_framework.citation: Law 2007-022 on Marriage + Law 2007-023 on Filiation
statutory_framework.title: Laws on Marriage and Filiation
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Laws on Marriage and Filiation reform.

statutory_framework.title: Law on Child Protection
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Law on Child Protection aligned with UNCRC obligations.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court (Cour Suprême)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: High Constitutional Court (Haute Cour Constitutionnelle)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Court with original jurisdiction over constitutional review.

professional_regulators.name: Ministry of Public Health, Madagascar
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Malagasy family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Madagascar independence + Tsiranana founding-presidency
key_developments.description: Madagascar independence 26 June 1960 from France — establishing presidential republic framework. Philibert Tsiranana first president 1960-1972 substantively significant founding-presidency framework. Foundational pre-1972-revolution framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: 1972 Madagascar revolution + Ramanantsoa + post-Tsiranana framework
key_developments.description: 1972 Madagascar revolution May 1972 substantively ending Tsiranana presidency — General Gabriel Ramanantsoa assumed leadership. Substantive political-institutional disruption affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Ratsiraka + Democratic Republic of Madagascar + Marxist-Leninist framework
key_developments.description: Didier Ratsiraka assumed presidency 21 December 1975 establishing Democratic Republic of Madagascar (Repoblika Demokratika Malagasy) under Marxist-Leninist framework. Substantively distinctive Indian Ocean African Marxist-Leninist framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Madagascar ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child + democratic-transition framework
key_developments.description: Madagascar ratified the UNCRC on 19 March 1991 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Substantive democratic-transition 1991-1993 establishing contemporary constitutional-democratic-trajectory. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration.

key_developments.title: Constitution of Madagascar 1992 + Third Republic framework
key_developments.description: Constitution of Madagascar 1992 adopted 19 August 1992 by referendum — substantively reforming political-institutional framework establishing Third Republic (subsequently amended 2010 establishing Fourth Republic). Substantive constitutional anchor for contemporary family-law jurisprudence.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 accession
key_developments.description: Madagascar acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 September 2004 — substantively significant Hague Indian Ocean accession affecting cross-border-jurisdiction-practice with France and other Hague-party states.

key_developments.title: Family-Law Reform Package — Laws 2007-022, 2007-023, 2007-024
key_developments.description: Federal Family-Law Reform Package 20 August 2007 — Law 2007-022 on Marriage, Law 2007-023 on Filiation, Law 2007-024 on Parental Authority enacted as comprehensive reform. Substantively distinctive within African corpus multi-statute family-law-reform approach. Arts. 1-30 of Law 2007-024 govern parental authority.

key_developments.title: 2009 Madagascar political crisis + Rajoelina-Ravalomanana succession + Fourth Republic 2010
key_developments.description: 2009 Madagascar political crisis January-March 2009 substantively disrupting political-institutional framework — Marc Ravalomanana resigned 17 March 2009. Andry Rajoelina assumed power 17 March 2009. Subsequent Constitution 2010 Fourth Republic establishing contemporary constitutional framework. Substantive AU-suspension 2009-2014.

key_developments.title: Law on Child Protection (Law 2017-014) + UNCRC-aligned framework
key_developments.description: Federal Law on Child Protection enacted Law 2017-014 aligned with UNCRC obligations. Substantively significant child-protection-framework consolidation within Indian Ocean civil-law framework.

key_developments.title: Rajoelina re-election + Médiateur de la République + 2030 fokontony framework
key_developments.description: Andry Rajoelina re-election 16 November 2023 substantively significant continuing-presidency framework. Médiateur de la République (Ombudsperson) institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Law 2007-024 + Law 2017-014 framework. Fokontony (traditional community council) institutional framework operating alongside formal jurisdiction. Substantively significant Indian Ocean institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Cour Suprême + Haute Cour Constitutionnelle — interest-of-the-child substantive register + Rajoelina continuing-presidency
key_developments.description: Cour Suprême and Haute Cour Constitutionnelle continue to develop interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Law 2007-024 + Law 2017-014 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes within Andry Rajoelina presidency (since 2019). Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Madagascar operates a French-civil-law family-law framework with substantial codification of customary-law and Merina/Betsileo traditional matrimonial-property provisions — places Madagascar in the Indian Ocean civil-law cluster.
Hague Convention 1980 accession 2004 places Madagascar in the Hague Indian Ocean cluster.
2007 family-law reform package (3 distinct Laws) is structurally distinctive within the African corpus — Madagascar's approach uses multiple dedicated statutes rather than single Family Code.
editorial_notes: Madagascar jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 3 to 10 key_developments with full Tsiranana-to-Rajoelina trajectory: 1960-Madagascar-independence-+-Tsiranana + 1972-1972-Madagascar-revolution-+-Ramanantsoa + 1975-Ratsiraka-+-Democratic-Republic-+-Marxist-Leninist + 1991-UNCRC-+-democratic-transition + 1992-Constitution-+-Third-Republic + 2004-Hague-Convention-1980-accession + 2007-Family-Law-Reform-Package-Laws-2007-022-023-024 + 2009-2009-Madagascar-political-crisis-+-Rajoelina-Ravalomanana + 2017-Law-on-Child-Protection-Law-2017-014 + 2024-Cour-Suprême-+-Haute-Cour-Constitutionnelle-+-Rajoelina-continuing-presidency.
Civil-law Indian Ocean (French civil-law substantive + customary-law codification + 2007 Family-Law Reform Package + Law on Child Protection 2017 + Hague Convention 1980 accession 2004).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive interest-of-the-child analysis under Law 2007-024 + Law 2017-014 + Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Indian-Ocean + civil-law + French-derivative + multi-statute-family-law-distinctive-vs-single-Family-Code + Merina-Betsileo-customary-law-codification + fitampoha-marriage-property-tradition + Ratsiraka-Marxist-Leninist-1975-1991-distinctive + 1972-Madagascar-revolution + 2009-political-crisis-+-AU-suspension + Hague-Convention-1980-accession-2004-Indian-Ocean clusters within the corpus.
